content:
As the title says, I released Setsuna, a cross-device text sharing service, on 12/28.
Setsuna - Real-time Text Sharing https://setsuna-text.vercel.app/ デバイス間テキスト共有アプリSetsunaをリリースしました。https://t.co/FNbVd8ohHOhttps://t.co/8VPNjyX0K7
昔自分用につくって公開していたOSSを、Claude Codeに全面バックアップしてもらってリバイバルしたものです。…
— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 28, 2025 (Since it was abbreviated, I’ll write it out here)
“This is a revival of an OSS I made for personal use a while back, with full backing from Claude Code. There are plenty of similar tools out there now and it’s nothing special, but I wanted to rebuild it once with modern tech as a learning exercise, so I released it.”
デバイス間テキスト共有サービスSetsunahttps://t.co/hjaJEAeu8ehttps://t.co/8VPNjyX0K7
バージョンは1.3.3になり、大体作りたい機能の開発が終わりました。
自力だと体感1ヶ月程度の実装が、Claude Codeのおかげで丸2日くらいで終わりました。確かに作業効率10倍くらいにはなってそうです。
— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 29, 2025 Originally it was a personal project of the same name, built with (if I remember correctly) Node + Express + Socket.IO and Vue… something like that. But I couldn’t fix some minor bugs, and leaving something incomplete that I couldn’t maintain didn’t sit right with me, so I took it down.
Thanks to Claude Code, the result far exceeded the original in quality, design, and maintainability. And above all, the development speed. I started on the 27th, released v1.0.0 on the 28th, and completed all planned features (plus some extras) with the v1.3.3 release on the 29th. At this level, it really feels like there’s no place for humans anymore (especially in the coding phase).
I’d like to keep improving it whenever good feature ideas come up.

content:
I read “Introduction to AI-Driven Development with Claude Code” (Claude Code によるAI駆動開発入門).
I shared some of my thoughts on X.
池袋に行ったのでジュンク堂でClaude CodeによるAI駆動開発入門、買いました。これはもういい意味で教科書ですね。使ったことないひとは体系的に学べる、使ったことあるひとはパラパラめくるだけで何かしら発見があるようなイメージ。 pic.twitter.com/fUPNrjwPQz
— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 10, 2025 既にほとんど知っている事柄に関して、他の人の異なるアプローチや考え方にふれる、記事や書籍を読む、ってのは本当によいことで。
新たな知識は増えないけど、代わりに新たなインスピレーションや気付きは発生するのでとても大事にしている。当然理解も深まり言語化もうまくなる。
— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) December 20, 2025 I also spoke about how great this book is to several people at the Claude Code Meetup Tokyo I recently attended.
Claude Code Meetup Tokyo · Luma https://luma.com/ioed4ksj?tk=U0Wvfo Beyond covering the basics of Claude Code in a textbook-like manner, what makes this book really stand out is that you can see what the author, Hirakawa-san, instructs Claude Code to do for a given requirement, and understand “what they’re thinking.” Even if you already know Claude Code well, reading from this perspective will teach you a lot. I certainly learned a great deal.
And I was fortunate enough to meet the author Hirakawa-san in person and have a conversation! What left a particularly strong impression was how they described their own book as being like DUO 3.0.
DUO 3.0 is a famous English vocabulary and phrase study book in Japan. While it can be used as a “vocabulary list,” its example sentences are excellent — by studying the sentences, you learn word meanings, usage, idioms, and set phrases all at once. In other words, the book is structured to help you learn English very efficiently.
I think “Introduction to AI-Driven Development with Claude Code” truly achieves learning efficiency similar to DUO 3.0. It’s not just a command reference or a collection of tips — you can see what prompts the author writes and how they interact with Claude Code while working on real tasks. Through these examples, you naturally pick up how to write prompts and develop your thinking process.
And just as DUO 3.0 isn’t a book you read once and put away, but rather one you keep at hand and refer to repeatedly, this book also helps you gradually sharpen your skills as you continue using Claude Code and keep coming back to it. It truly has value as a “practical textbook for development with Claude Code.”
I highly recommend it — both for those just starting with Claude Code and for experienced users who will surely find new insights.
Claude Code によるAI駆動開発入門 Claude Codeの基本から活用方法までこの一冊でぜんぶ学べる！ Amazon.co.jp
summary:

Beyond covering the basics of Claude Code in a textbook-like manner, what makes this book really stand out is that you can see what the author, Hirakawa-san, instructs Claude Code to do for a given requirement, and understand “what they’re thinking.” Even if you already know Claude Code well, reading from this perspective will teach you a lot. I certainly learned a great deal.

content:
The Site…? Yes.
I write technical articles on Zenn, so this is a place for everything that doesn’t belong there.
QUIET QUALIA That’s the name of the site.
There’s probably no deep meaning behind it. Maybe.
How Was It Built? Hugo + PaperMod
Hugo is great because it’s easy to customize. And if you get stuck, you can just ask an AI.
Main Customizations (Notes) Hatena Bookmark share button Zenn-style author block (below articles) Author icon I think that’s about it…?
Deployment ブログつくってて
ローカル→GitHub→GitHub Action→ホスティングサーバー
と思ったけど、GitHub Action書きたくないので
ローカル→GitHub &&
ローカル→SSH→ホスティングサーバーからgit pull
をdeploy\.shに書いて自動化でよさげ
GitHub経由する意味があまりないのは気のせいなはず
— びーぐる (@beagle_dog_inu) November 24, 2025 There’s a non-zero chance I might migrate servers, so having it on GitHub is definitely the right call.
Future Plans Create an About page Create a Projects page Adjust fonts and line spacing
summary:
The Site…?

Yes.
I write technical articles on Zenn, so this is a place for everything that doesn’t belong there.

QUIET QUALIA

That’s the name of the site.
There’s probably no deep meaning behind it. Maybe.

How Was It Built?

Hugo + PaperMod
Hugo is great because it’s easy to customize. And if you get stuck, you can just ask an AI.

Main Customizations (Notes)

  • Hatena Bookmark share button
  • Zenn-style author block (below articles)
  • Author icon

I think that’s about it…?
title: The Site

Me Name Beagle
Overview AI Enthusiast
Freelance Software Engineer
Tech Writer, Indie Developer
Based in Saitama, Japan — born in Sapporo
Contact X: @beagle_dog_inu GitHub: beagleworks Zenn: beagle Career / Work Business Name Beagle Works
Career Working as a freelancer since university
Skill Set Development & Operations Technology selection & architecture design General software development Full-stack web application development System operations, maintenance & security AI Planning & developing LLM-powered applications Development with coding agents & organizational adoption AI adoption support, promotion & training for organizations Management & Business Engineering management Product management (apprentice) Writing & Speaking Writing tech books & articles Speaking at AI-related events (LTs, 15-min sessions, talks) Current Interests Research on SDD (Spec-Driven Development) Research on improving SDLC and AI workflows Keeping up with Claude Code and Codex Indie development (mostly for personal use) Writing technical articles Networking & speaking at offline events & meetups, attending #aimeetup events Certifications Network Specialist Information Security Management Examination (registered 2017–2023) Work History Development & operations of event production web applications (2014–2026?) Support for transitioning to fully remote work environments (2020) PoC development with LLMs, planning & development of LLM-based productivity apps (2024, 2025) New development of business applications (multiple) Website creation, operations & maintenance (multiple) Tech book writing (published in Software Design, February 2026 issue) …and more. Some can be shared publicly — may introduce them later.
Speaking History March 3, 2026 — Vibe Coding Feature from Software Design Feb. Issue hosted by Work≠Build (invited) March 19, 2026 — Codex Meetup Tokyo #1 hosted by AIAU — LT (proposal accepted) Events Requiring Approval to Attend December 22, 2025 — Claude Code Meetup Tokyo hosted by #aimeetup Speaking Attempts October 2025 — LT on indie development (event not held) October 2025 — Session on Sora prompt techniques (event cancelled) 2025 — Talk on Spec-Driven Development (event cancelled?) February 2026 — “Deep Dive into Claude Code” LT meetup hosted by Findy, Inc. (proposal rejected) March 2026 — Claude Code Meetup Japan #3 hosted by AI-Driven Dev Study Group (proposal rejected) Personal Hobbies I don’t have that many hobbies, which makes it awkward when asked
Keeping up with IT technology Writing Programming (AtCoder blue rated) PC building Collecting small figures (Dragon Quest monster mini figures, etc.) Shogi (casual player) Watching horse racing (as a sport, not gambling) Family Wife, two daughters
Fun Facts I know plenty of great spots in Hokkaido, so feel free to ask when planning a trip!
(Though things have changed so much lately that I’m losing confidence…)
For work, writing, or speaking inquiries, please contact info_0@beagleworks.net or reach out via X DMs.
The email address above is changed periodically, so please check this page instead of saving it to your address book.

title: About

content:
This Privacy Policy describes how personal information is handled on this website (QUIET QUALIA).
Operator Information Site Name: QUIET QUALIA Operator: Beagle (Beagle Works) URL: https://blog.beagleworks.net Collection of Personal Information This website does not have contact forms or comment features, and does not directly collect personal information from visitors.
Additionally, no analytics tools (such as Google Analytics) are used.
Amazon Associates Program This site is a participant in the Amazon Associates Program, an affiliate advertising program designed to provide a means for sites to earn referral fees by advertising and linking to Amazon.co.jp.
Product links in articles on this site may include Amazon Associates links. If you purchase a product through these links, this site may receive a referral fee, but no additional cost is incurred by the purchaser.
Cookies This website itself does not use cookies; however, the Amazon Associates Program may cause cookies to be used on Amazon’s side.
Cookies are small text files stored in your browser when you visit a website. Amazon uses cookies to track visitor activity, display relevant advertisements, and calculate referral fees.
If you do not wish to use cookies, you can disable them in your browser settings. However, some features may not function properly in that case.
Disclaimer While every effort is made to ensure the accuracy of information published on this site, we do not guarantee its content.
The operator assumes no responsibility for any damages arising from the use of information on this site.
The operator assumes no responsibility for information or services provided on sites accessed through links or banners on this site.
Contact For inquiries about this site, please contact us through the following social media account:
X (Twitter): @beagle_dog_inu Changes to This Privacy Policy This site may modify this Privacy Policy as necessary. The updated Privacy Policy shall take effect upon publication on this page.
Established: November 1, 2025
